channel.title: QuCS Lectures
channel.description: Quantum Computing Society lecture series - exploring quantum computing from theory to practice

item.title: 1 | Software and Algorithmic Approaches to Quantum Noise Mitigation: An Overview
item.description: I will talk about recent developments in noise mitigation techniques for quantum computers. In the Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) era, qubits have short lifetimes and quantum gates are prone to errors. This talk will provide an overview of software and algorithmic approaches to mitigate quantum noise.

item.title: 10 | Solving Nonlinear Partial Differential Equations using Variational Quantum Algorithms on Noisy Quantum Computers
item.description: Partial differential equations (PDEs) have long been the center of interest to system modeling in many disciplines of science and engineering, such as computational physics, fluid mechanics, and quantitative finance.

item.title: 12 | Quantum Computer Hardware Cybersecurity
item.description: As Quantum Computer device research continues to advance rapidly, there are also advances at the other levels of the computer system stack that involve these devices.

item.title: 11 | Building Blocks of Scalable Quantum Information Science
item.description: Quantum information technologies are expected to enable transformative technologies with wide-ranging global impact.

item.title: 14 | Compilation for Near-Term Quantum Computing: Gap Analysis and Optimal Solution
item.description: The most challenging stage in compilation for near-term quantum computing is qubit mapping, also called layout synthesis, where qubits in quantum programs are mapped to physical qubits.

item.title: 15 | Quantum Crosstalk Robust Quantum Control
item.description: The prevalence of quantum crosstalk in current quantum devices poses challenges to achieving high-fidelity quantum logic operations and reliable quantum processing.

item.title: 16 | Distributed Quantum Computing
item.description: Quantum processing units (QPUs) have to satisfy highly demanding quantity and quality requirements on their qubits to produce accurate results for problems at useful scales.

item.title: 18 | Enabling Deeper Quantum Compiler Optimization at High Level
item.description: A quantum compiler is one essential and critical component in a quantum computing system to deploy and optimize the quantum programs onto the underlying physical quantum hardware platforms.

item.title: 17 | Developing Robust System Software Support for Quantum Computers
item.description: The field of quantum computing has observed extraordinary advances in the last decade, including the design and engineering of quantum computers with more than a hundred qubits.

item.title: 13 | Enabling robust quantum computer system by understanding errors from NISQ machines
item.description: The growth of the need for quantum computers in many domains such as machine learning, numerical scientific simulation, and finance has urged quantum computers to produce more stable and less error-prone results.

item.title: 19 | Classical Verification of Quantum Depth
item.description: Verifying if a remote server has sufficient quantum resources to demonstrate quantum advantage is a fascinating question in complexity theory as well as a practical challenge.

item.title: 2 | A Guided Tour on the Map of Quantum Computing
item.description: A lecture by Zhixin Song on quantum computing.

item.title: 20 | Learning and Training in Quantum Environments
item.description: Quantum computing presents fascinating new opportunities for various applications, including machine learning, simulation, and optimization.

item.title: 21 | Quantum Machine Learning: Theoretical Foundations and Applications on NISQ Devices
item.description: Quantum machine learning (QML) is a trailblazing research subject that integrates quantum computing and machine learning.

item.title: 22 | Noise Modeling of the IBM Quantum Experience
item.description: The influence of noise in quantum dynamics is one of the main factors preventing Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) devices from performing useful quantum computations.

item.title: 23 | Software Tools for Analog Quantum Computing
item.description: Recent experimental results suggest that continuous-time analog quantum simulation would be advantageous over gate-based digital quantum simulation in the Noisy Intermediate-Size Quantum (NISQ) machine era.

item.title: 24 | Automatic Formal Verification of the Qiskit Compiler
item.description: Quantum compilers are essential in the quantum software stack but are error-prone.

item.title: 25 | QDiff: Differential Testing for Quantum Software Stacks
item.description: Several quantum software stacks (QSS) have been developed in response to rapid hardware advances in quantum computing.

item.title: 26 | Abstractions Are Bridges Toward Quantum Programming
item.description: In this talk, I present abstractions that help classical developers reason about the quantum world, with the goal of designing expressive and sound tools for quantum programming.

item.title: 27 | Understanding Quantum Supremacy Conditions for Gaussian Boson Sampling with High Performance Computing
item.description: Recent quantum supremacy experiments demonstrated with boson sampling garnered significant attention, while efforts to perfect approximate classical simulation techniques challenge supremacy claims on different fronts.

item.title: 28 | Pulse Control for Superconducting Quantum Computers
item.description: In superconducting quantum computer, quantum gates are compiled down to a sequence of microwave pulses.

item.title: 29 | Rethinking Most-significant Digit-first Arithmetic for Quantum Computing in NISQ Era
item.description: In recent years, quantum computers have attracted extensive research interest due to their potential capability of solving problems which are not easily solvable using classical computers.

item.title: 3 | Introduction to Variational Quantum Algorithms
item.description: A lecture by Jinglei Cheng on quantum computing.

item.title: 30 | Efficient Hamiltonian Reduction for Scalable Quantum Computing on Clique Cover/Graph Coloring Problems in SatCom
item.description: Clique cover and graph coloring are complementary problems which have many applications in wireless communications, especially in satellite communications (SatCom).

item.title: 32 | Hoare logic for verification of quantum programs
item.description: Quantum computing and quantum communication provide potential speed-up and enhanced security compared with their classical counterparts.

item.title: 31 | Quantum Computing and Quantum Communication in the Financial World
item.description: Finance has been identified as the first industry sector to benefit from quantum computing, due to its abundance of use cases with exponential complexity and the fact that, in finance, time is of the essence, which makes the case for solutions to be computed with high accuracy in real time.

item.title: 34 | Optimize Quantum Learning on Near-Term Noisy Quantum Computers
item.description: In recent years, there has been a significant breakthrough in the development of superconducting quantum computers, with IBM’s 433-qubit quantum computer being a prime example of the progress made in addressing scalability issues.

item.title: 33 | Quantum Graphical Calculi: Tutorial and Applications
item.description: Quantum computing and quantum communication provide potential speed-up and enhanced security compared with their classical counterparts.

item.title: 35 | Hybrid Quantum-Classic Computing for Future Network Optimization
item.description: Benefited from the technology development of controlling quantum particles and constructing quantum hardware, quantum computation has attracted more and more attention in recent years.

item.title: 36 | Hybrid Quantum-Classical Machine Learning with Applications
item.description: The development of machine learning (ML) and quantum computing (QC) hardware has generated a lot of interest in creating quantum machine learning (QML) applications.

item.title: 37 | Elevating Quantum Compiler Performance through Enhanced Awareness in the Compilation Stages
item.description: Quantum compiler plays a critical role in practical quantum compilation, particularly in the Noise-Intermediate-Scale-Quantum (NISQ) era.

item.title: 38 | Pulse-based Variational Quantum Eigensolver and Pulse-Efficient Transpilation
item.description: State-of-the-art noisy digital quantum computers can only execute short-depth quantum circuits.

item.title: 39 | Control Systems & Systems Software @ IBM Quantum
item.description: The field of quantum computing has rapidly developed around a cloud model, in which users receive a remote handle to a quantum system, compile (transpile) quantum programs (circuits) for the target system, and then remotely invoke an execution on the system from which they then fetch the results of.

item.title: 4 | Enabling Parallel Circuit Execution on NISQ Hardware
item.description: Today’s quantum computers are in the Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum era and prone to errors.

item.title: 40 | Parameter Setting in Quantum Approximate Optimization of Weighted Problems
item.description: Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) is a leading candidate algorithm for solving combinatorial optimization problems on quantum computers.

item.title: 41 | The Nonequilibrium Cost of Accuracy
item.description: Accurate information processing is crucial both in technology and in nature.

item.title: 42 | Align or Not Align? Design Quantum Approximate Operator Ansatz (QAOA) with Applications in Constrained Optimization
item.description: Combinatorial optimization has been one of most promising use cases of the near-term quantum computers.

item.title: 43 | A Structured Method for Compilation of QAOA Circuits in Quantum Computing
item.description: Zheng (Eddy) Zhang is an Associate Professor at Rutgers University.

item.title: 44 | Quantum-Inspired Optimization for Real-World Trading by Icosa powered by gBraid
item.description: Quantum computing lecture from the QuCS series.

item.title: 45 | Enabling High Performance Debugging for Variational Quantum Algorithms using Compressed Sensing
item.description: Quantum computing lecture from the QuCS series.

item.title: 46 | Numerical Methods of Optimal Quantum Control
item.description: At the heart of the next-level quantum technology like quantum computing lies the problem of actively controlling the dynamics of a quantum system.

item.title: 48 | Quantum Circuit Decomposition and Routing Collaborative Design
item.description: Quantum computing lecture from the QuCS series.

item.title: 47 | Quantum Machine Learning on Current Quantum Computers
item.description: Quantum computing lecture from the QuCS series.

item.title: 49 | Quantum Reservoir Engineering for Fast and Scalable Entanglement Stabilization
item.description: High-fidelity entanglement is a prerequisite for almost any quantum information processing task.

item.title: 5 | Design Automation and Software Tools for Quantum Computing
item.description: Quantum computing is becoming a reality, but automated methods and software tools for this technology are just beginning.

item.title: 50 | Quantum Oracle Synthesis with an Application to QRNG
item.description: Several prominent quantum computing algorithms—including Grover’s search algorithm and Shor’s algorithm for finding the prime factorization of an integer—employ subcircuits termed ‘oracles’ that embed a specific instance of a mathematical function into a corresponding bijective function that is then realized as a quantum circuit representation.

item.title: 51 | Design Automation for Quantum Computing
item.description: This talk will provide the full picture of design automation for quantum computing - from designing quantum algorithms and quantum circuits to quantum computing using physical quantum hardware.

item.title: 52 | Open Quantum Systems in Quantum Computing
item.description: Quantum dynamics in real-world scenarios seldom stand alone; they occur under the continuous influence of surrounding environments.

item.title: 53 | Quantum Computational Chemistry: Variational Quantum Eigensolver and the Design of Ansatz
item.description: Over the past decade, quantum computing has experienced remarkable growth and development, opening up new possibilities for simulating molecular properties.

item.title: 54 | Quantum Computing Systems: Challenges and Opportunities
item.description: The second quantum revolution, the transition from quantum theory to quantum engineering, is leading us toward practical quantum computing.

item.title: 55 | Scaling Quantum Computing Systems via Codesign
item.description: The full promise of quantum computation will only be realized if quantum devices scale.

item.title: 56 | ODEgen: Analytic Gradients of Quantum Pulse Programs
item.description: We introduce ODEgen, a new method for evaluating analytic gradients of quantum pulse programs and contrast it with the stochastic parameter-shift rule.

item.title: 57 | Numerical Quantum Control: Scalable Gate Design and Calibration
item.description: The goal of numerical quantum control is to automate the gate design process, with the promise of rapid and flexible gate design for arbitrary systems.

item.title: 58 | Variational Quantum Semi-definite Programming
item.description: Solving optimization problems is a key task for which quantum computers could possibly provide a speedup over the best known classical algorithms.

item.title: 59 | Achieve Explanable Quantum Computing Using Visualization
item.description: Quantum computing has made significant progress in recent years.

item.title: 6 | Adaptive Online Learning of Quantum States
item.description: Shadow tomography is a fundamental problem in quantum computing, whose goal is to efficiently learn an unknown d-dimensional quantum state using projective measurements.

item.title: 60 | Quantum algorithms for simulating the dynamics of open quantum systems
item.description: Quantum computing lecture from the QuCS series.

item.title: 61 | Efficient Quantum Verification Approaches.
item.description: Quantum computing lecture from the QuCS series.

item.title: 62 | Multimodal Learning for Cross-Platform Verification in Early-Stage Quantum Computing
item.description: Quantum computing lecture from the QuCS series.

item.title: 63 | Engineer’s Guide to Machine Learning with Quantum Computers
item.description: Quantum computing lecture from the QuCS series.

item.title: 64 | Decoder for quantum error correction codes: from the perspective of classical error correction codes
item.description: Quantum error correction has become a crucial and popular topic, especially given its essential role in ensuring the scalability and reliability of quantum computers.

item.title: 65 | Scaling Quantum Communication Networks
item.description: Quantum computing lecture from the QuCS series.

item.title: 66 | Coordinating Decisions via Quantum Telepathy
item.description: Quantum computing lecture from the QuCS series.

item.title: 67 | Quantum Recursive Programming: Verification and Implementation
item.description: Quantum recursive programming has recently been introduced for describing sophisticated and complicated quantum algorithms in a compact and elegant way.

item.title: 68 | Bridging Hardware and Software: Optimizing Quantum Compilation and Benchmarking for Scalable Quantum Systems
item.description: This lecture discusses how integrated hardware–software co-design can address key challenges in scalable quantum computing.

item.title: 69 | TQEC Tool Overview
item.description: Compiling a quantum algorithm to run on a quantum computer consisting of a 2D array of qubits with only nearest neighbor interactions is a complex problem.

item.title: 7 | Quantum Data Center
item.description: We propose the Quantum Data Center (QDC), an architecture combining Quantum Random Access Memory (QRAM) and quantum networks.

item.title: 70 | The Brave New (Quantum) World
item.description: Major advances across all the design stack of Quantum computing – algorithm, software, and hardware – has brought us to a realm where, it is impossible to ignore the effect of Quantum computing in the world around us.

item.title: 8 | Classical Support and Error Mitigation for Variational Quantum Algorithms
item.description: In this seminar Dr.

item.title: 9 | Protocol Design for Quantum Network Routing
item.description: Quantum entanglement enables important computing applications such as quantum key distribution.

item.title: 71 | Using Quantum Probability to Understand Contextual Reasoning
item.description: What kind of logic do people use to reason about natural events? What kind of probability theory best describes how people make inferences and decisions under uncertainty?
